summary: Tested Iter 94 sub-admin Menu Rights feature (frontend focus). Super-admin editor UX works end-to-end: 'Sidebar Menu Access (Web Portal)' section renders under Edit sub-admin modal, existing state loads (/salary-run visibly Blocked), toggling /tickets to Blocked persists to backend via PATCH and reloads correctly on reopen. Backend GET /admin/sub-admins and /auth/me both return menu_rights correctly. HOWEVER — the actual sidebar-filtering side of the feature is unreachable: sub-admins are hard-blocked from the web portal by AdminWebShell.tsx L476 which routes any role other than super_admin or company_admin to the EmployeeWebGate ('Please use the mobile app') screen. The sub_admin branch in `pickNav` (L387-415) is dead code until this guard is patched.

ui_bugs.issue: Sub-admin role is hard-gated out of the web portal. /app/frontend/src/components/AdminWebShell.tsx line 476: `if (role !== 'super_admin' && role !== 'company_admin') return <EmployeeWebGate />;` never allows sub_admin through — so the Menu Rights filtering added at lines 387-415 is unreachable. Reproduction: log in as testsub@sksharma.co / testsub123 on desktop web preview → user is dropped on the 'Please use the mobile app' screen regardless of menu_rights.

integration_issues.flow: Sub-admin sidebar restriction (end-user side of feature)
integration_issues.issue: Cannot verify because sub_admin never reaches the shell. Once the EmployeeWebGate guard is patched to also allow 'sub_admin', the existing filter code should apply menu_rights (verified via /auth/me: {'/salary-run': false, '/reports': false, '/tickets': false, '/employees': true}).

design_issues.screen: Edit sub-admin modal — Sidebar Menu Access section
issues: The parent 'Reports' nav group in NAV_SUPER has no route (only children like /reports?tab=salary), so setting menu_rights['/reports']=false via API has no effect on the sidebar. The Menu Rights toggles list only shows children rows. This is a minor semantic mismatch — main agent seeded {'/reports': false} but the UI has no matching toggle and the sub-admin sidebar would still show Reports children. Consider either (a) adding a synthetic parent toggle that cascades to all children of a group, or (b) documenting that only leaf routes are supported.
Similarly menu_rights['/employees'] is not a NAV_SUPER route (nav uses '/admin' for Employee Master Data), so it is inert.

action_items: CRITICAL: Patch /app/frontend/src/components/AdminWebShell.tsx line 476 to include sub_admin, e.g.: `if (role !== 'super_admin' && role !== 'company_admin' && role !== 'sub_admin') return <EmployeeWebGate />;`. Without this, the sub-admin sidebar restriction feature is dead code.

After patch, re-verify: log in as testsub@sksharma.co / testsub123 on desktop web → sidebar should render and Salary Process (Actual), Reports children (?tab=salary/compliance/bonus), Tickets should NOT appear; Dashboard, Add New Employee, Companies etc. should appear.

Minor: consider adding parent-group toggles for Reports/Utilities/Automation groups (or hide/short-circuit if all children blocked), and drop the inert '/reports' and '/employees' legacy keys from the API seed.
critical_code_review_comments: AdminWebShell.tsx L476 guard was not updated when the sub_admin sidebar-filter branch (L387-415) was added — classic feature-with-unreachable-code regression.

SUB_ADMIN_ALWAYS_BLOCKED set in /app/frontend/app/sub-admins.tsx correctly hides admin-only routes from the Menu Rights toggles list (verified: /sub-admins, /employer-access-rights, /super-admin-access, /attendance-sheet, /masters, /compliance-policy, /portal-automation, /ai-insights, /appearance all absent from toggle list).
testIDs sa-menu-<route> generated with `route.replace(/[^a-z0-9]/gi,'_')` correctly (e.g. /salary-run -> sa-menu-_salary_run, /reports?tab=salary -> sa-menu-_reports_tab_salary). Confirmed working.

success_rate.frontend: 50% (super-admin editor works; sub-admin restriction blocked by unrelated web-gate bug)

seed_data_creation: None. Used existing sub_623b8a106846 (Test SubAdmin). Note: side effect of this test run — /tickets is now also Blocked in menu_rights for that sub-admin (was toggled during persistence verification).
context_for_next_testing_agent: After main agent patches AdminWebShell.tsx L476, log in via /admin-pin-login (auto-selects password mode on web) as testsub@sksharma.co / testsub123. Note password_must_change=true — but current admin-pin-login flow does not force the set-password screen and just navigates to /. Verify sidebar hides: Salary Process (Actual), all Reports children, Tickets. Verify sidebar shows: Dashboard, Add New Employee, Companies, Employee Master Data, Compliance Salary, Bulk Employee Correction, Approvals children, Utilities children, Bonus Process, Automation children (Email/Challans), Employee Groups/Shifts, Bulk Import, Attendance Policy, Users Log Report, Messages. Note: sub-admins are also hard-blocked from /sub-admins /employer-access-rights /super-admin-access /attendance-sheet /masters /compliance-policy /portal-automation /ai-insights /appearance regardless of menu_rights.
rca_of_issue: RCA: The Iter 94 feature added menu_rights persistence + editor UI + sub_admin sidebar filter, but AdminWebShell.tsx has a pre-existing web-gate at L476 that only whitelists super_admin and company_admin — sub_admin falls into the else branch and gets the EmployeeWebGate. Mitigation: one-line change to include 'sub_admin' in the whitelist. Verified in code that the sidebar-filter branch for sub_admin at L387-415 is correctly implemented and would execute once the gate is opened.
